steps.explanation: The equation $d = 16t$ is a linear relationship. In the form $d = rt$, the coefficient of $t$ represents the rate at which $d$ changes per unit of $t$. Here $d$ is in inches and $t$ is in seconds, so the coefficient has units of inches per second.
When $t = 1$, $d = 16$. When $t = 2$, $d = 32$. Each additional second adds exactly 16 inches, confirming 16 is the speed of the object.

Evaluating all choices:
  • Choice A — ‘The object moved a total of $16$ inches.’: "The object moved a total of 16 inches." Incorrect — the total distance depends on $t$ and is $16t$, not a fixed 16.
  • Choice B — ‘The object moved a total of $16t$ inches.’: "The object moved a total of $16t$ inches." Incorrect — $16t$ describes the full expression for $d$, not the interpretation of 16 alone.
  • Choice C — ‘The object is moving at a rate of $16$ inches per second.’: "The object is moving at a rate of 16 inches per second." Correct — the coefficient 16 is the rate of change of distance with respect to time. That confirms C is correct.
  • Choice D — ‘The object is moving at a rate of $\frac{1}{16}$ inches per second.’: "The object is moving at a rate of $\frac{1}{16}$ inches per second." Incorrect — this would be the rate if the equation were $d = \frac{t}{16}$.
